baseline:
Security steering active. Always implement secure-by-default:
- Treat all input as untrusted
- Enforce authentication and least privilege
- Never hardcode or expose secrets
- Use secure defaults
- Prevent common vulnerabilities
- Do not suggest insecure shortcuts

auth.guidance: Authentication & session guidance: short-lived tokens with rotation on refresh; validate issuer/audience/signature/expiry on every JWT check; MFA for admin paths; Secure+HttpOnly+SameSite=Strict on session cookies.

injection.guidance: Injection guidance: parameterized queries only; validate input at the boundary (schema + type + bounds); escape output per sink (HTML / shell / SQL / URL).

crypto.guidance: Cryptography guidance: Argon2id or bcrypt (cost >= 12) for passwords; AES-256-GCM with CSPRNG IVs for symmetric; TLS 1.2+ on external surfaces; rotate keys, never hardcode.

xss_csrf.guidance: Browser & output-encoding guidance: escape untrusted input before rendering; CSP with script-src 'self' + nonce/hash (no unsafe-inline); SameSite=Strict cookies + anti-CSRF tokens on state-changing requests; restrictive CORS origins. Never eval/exec untrusted data.

secrets.guidance: Secrets guidance: never commit secrets to the repo; read from the organisation's secret store (Vault/KMS/SSM) at runtime; no secrets in logs, client-side code, or error messages.

iac.guidance: Container & IaC guidance: non-root user; drop all capabilities then add only needed ones; pin image digests (no :latest); read-only filesystem where possible; no --privileged; secrets via mounts, not env vars, in K8s.

llm.guidance: LLM surface guidance (OWASP LLM Top 10): treat model input as untrusted (LLM01); never exec/eval model output unchecked (LLM02); filter PII/secrets before sending to the model (LLM06).
